steps: Fill in your application name, company name, website URL, and redirect URI
Submit the form to receive your Client ID and Client Secret
Use the authorization URL https://launchpad.37signals.com/authorization/new to start the OAuth flow
After the user authorizes, exchange the code at https://launchpad.37signals.com/authorization/token
Use the access token as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' in API requests to https://3.basecampapi.com/{account_id}/

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: The OAuth Client ID for your registered Basecamp application

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: The OAuth Client Secret for your registered Basecamp application

notes: Basecamp uses OAuth 2.0 only — no API key option
Register your app at https://launchpad.37signals.com/integrations (37signals developer portal)
The Basecamp 3 API base URL is https://3.basecampapi.com/{account_id}/
You can get the account ID from the authorization response or the /authorization.json endpoint
Basecamp requires a User-Agent header identifying your app and contact email
30-day free trial available; paid plans start at $15/month

test_endpoint.description: Get authorization info including account IDs for the authenticated user
